title: Residential HVAC System Overview
fastAnswer: A residential HVAC system controls indoor climate, ensuring comfort through heating, ventilation, and air conditioning.

mostCommonFix.title: Replace Air Filter
mostCommonFix.summary: Replacing the air filter can improve airflow and efficiency, preventing common issues like restricted airflow.

causes.name: Dirty Air Filter
causes.description: Reduces airflow, causing the system to work harder and less efficiently.

causes.name: Refrigerant Leak
causes.description: Leads to insufficient cooling as the system cannot maintain the required pressure.

causes.name: Faulty Thermostat
causes.description: Prevents the system from maintaining the desired temperature.

repairOptions.name: Seal Refrigerant Leak
repairOptions.description: Restores proper refrigerant levels and cooling efficiency.

ignoredConsequences: Ignoring minor issues can lead to major system failures and increased energy bills.
technicianInsights: Regular maintenance is key to preventing common HVAC issues and extending system lifespan.
uniqueElement: In humid climates, regular checks for mold and mildew in the system are crucial.

title: AC Not Cooling
fastAnswer: If your AC is not cooling, check the thermostat, air filter, and refrigerant levels first.

mostCommonFix.title: Replace Air Filter
mostCommonFix.summary: A clogged air filter can restrict airflow, causing the AC to not cool effectively.

diagnosticFlow: Check and replace air filter if dirty

causes.name: Refrigerant Leak
causes.description: Causes the system to lose cooling capacity.

causes.name: Faulty Compressor
causes.description: Prevents the system from circulating refrigerant effectively.

ignoredConsequences: Ignoring cooling issues can lead to higher energy bills and potential system breakdown.
technicianInsights: Addressing minor issues promptly can prevent costly repairs and extend the life of your AC unit.
uniqueElement: In areas with high pollen counts, air filters may clog faster, requiring more frequent replacement.

title: How to Diagnose Warm Air from AC
fastAnswer: To diagnose warm air from your AC, check the thermostat, filter, and refrigerant levels.

mostCommonFix.title: Replace Air Filter
mostCommonFix.summary: A clean air filter can improve airflow and cooling efficiency.

diagnosticFlow: Set thermostat to 'cool' and desired temperature
Check air filter for dirt and replace if necessary
Inspect outdoor unit for debris or obstructions

causes.name: Refrigerant Leak
causes.description: Reduces system's ability to cool effectively.

causes.name: Dirty Air Filter
causes.description: Restricts airflow, causing warm air output.

ignoredConsequences: Ignoring warm air issues can lead to increased energy consumption and discomfort.
technicianInsights: Regular checks and maintenance can prevent warm air issues and improve system efficiency.
uniqueElement: In coastal areas, salt air can corrode outdoor units, requiring more frequent inspections.

title: Refrigerant Leak
fastAnswer: A refrigerant leak reduces the AC's ability to cool effectively and can cause system damage if not addressed.

mostCommonFix.title: Seal Refrigerant Leak
mostCommonFix.summary: Sealing the leak and recharging refrigerant restores system pressure and cooling capacity.

diagnosticFlow: Listen for hissing sounds near lines
Inspect for oily residue on components
Use a refrigerant gauge to check pressure
Check for ice buildup on evaporator coils

causes.name: Corroded Coils
causes.description: Corrosion can cause small holes, leading to leaks.

causes.name: Loose Fittings
causes.description: Vibrations can loosen fittings, causing leaks.

causes.name: Factory Defects
causes.description: Manufacturing defects can lead to premature leaks.

repairOptions.name: Seal Refrigerant Leak
repairOptions.description: Stops leak and restores cooling efficiency.

ignoredConsequences: Ignoring a refrigerant leak can lead to compressor failure and increased energy costs.
technicianInsights: Early detection of refrigerant leaks can prevent costly repairs and extend system life.
uniqueElement: In older homes, outdated piping may increase the risk of leaks.

title: Seal Refrigerant Leak
fastAnswer: Sealing a refrigerant leak involves locating the leak, sealing it, and recharging the system with refrigerant.

mostCommonFix.title: Seal Refrigerant Leak
mostCommonFix.summary: Sealing the leak and recharging refrigerant restores system pressure and cooling capacity.

diagnosticFlow: Use a leak detector to find the leak
Seal the leak using appropriate sealant
Recharge the system with the correct refrigerant
Check system pressure with a gauge

causes.name: Corroded Coils
causes.description: Corrosion can cause small holes, leading to leaks.

causes.name: Loose Fittings
causes.description: Vibrations can loosen fittings, causing leaks.

causes.name: Factory Defects
causes.description: Manufacturing defects can lead to premature leaks.

repairOptions.name: Seal Refrigerant Leak
repairOptions.description: Stops leak and restores cooling efficiency.

ignoredConsequences: Attempting to seal a refrigerant leak without proper tools can lead to further damage.
technicianInsights: Professional repair is recommended to ensure proper sealing and system recharging.
uniqueElement: In high-altitude areas, pressure differences can affect refrigerant behavior.

title: AC Repair in Tampa
fastAnswer: In Tampa, AC systems often face issues due to high humidity and salt air, requiring regular maintenance.

mostCommonFix.title: Replace Air Filter
mostCommonFix.summary: Regularly replacing air filters can prevent common issues caused by Tampa's humid climate.

diagnosticFlow: Inspect for mold growth in ducts
Check for corrosion on outdoor unit

causes.name: Mold Growth
causes.description: High humidity fosters mold, affecting air quality and system efficiency.

causes.name: Salt Corrosion
causes.description: Salt air can corrode components, leading to leaks and failures.

causes.name: Clogged Drain Lines
causes.description: Humidity can cause drain lines to clog, leading to water damage.

repairOptions.name: Replace Air Filter
repairOptions.description: Prevents airflow restrictions and improves air quality.

repairOptions.name: Clean Mold from Ducts
repairOptions.description: Improves air quality and system efficiency.

ignoredConsequences: Ignoring maintenance in Tampa's climate can lead to costly repairs and reduced system lifespan.
technicianInsights: Regular maintenance is crucial in Tampa to combat the effects of humidity and salt air.
uniqueElement: Tampa's coastal environment accelerates wear on outdoor units, making regular inspections essential.
